meanings.usage: 通常修饰工作、劳动等需要体力完成的活动
用于描述从事非体力办公室工作的人群或相关属性
常用搭配sacrifice sth for sth，表为了某物舍弃另一物
可指社会地位带来的特殊优待，也可表荣幸做某事
除表好奇的，还可修饰事物表示不寻常、值得注意的
英式英语表述，对应美式英语garbage man
用来描述人因处境尴尬、害羞的状态，修饰事物用embarrassing
常用复数形式，指工作时穿的防护性服装
放在名词前，指和自己有相同身份、处境的人
指个人在社会、组织中的层级地位，不可数名词

phrases.usage: 将某人称作、把某人称为，用来表达对某人的称谓

examples.en: People who work in offices are frequently referred to as 'white-collar workers'.
examples.zh: 在办公室工作的人常常被称作“白领工人”。

examples.en: He is often referred to as the best basketball player of his generation.
examples.zh: 他常被称为他那一代人中最好的篮球运动员。

phrases.usage: 引起；导致；使发生，后接表示结果类的名词

examples.en: This can give rise to curious situations.
examples.zh: 这就可能引起一些奇怪的现象。

examples.en: Poor communication may give rise to misunderstandings between team members.
examples.zh: 沟通不畅可能会导致团队成员之间产生误会。

phrases.usage: 就……而言；在……的情况下，用来引出具体的例子或情况

examples.en: As it did in the case of Alfred Bloggs who worked as a dustman.
examples.zh: 在阿尔弗雷德·布洛格斯的情况中就是如此，他是埃尔斯米尔公司的清洁工。

examples.en: The new policy is quite flexible, especially in the case of small businesses.
examples.zh: 这项新政策很灵活，对小企业来说尤其如此。

phrases.usage: 太……而不能……，用来表达否定含义，说明程度过高导致无法做某事

examples.en: Alf was too embarrassed to say anything to his wife about his job.
examples.zh: 阿尔弗太尴尬了，以至于没法和妻子说自己工作的事。

examples.en: The little boy was too short to reach the book on the top shelf.
examples.zh: 这个小男孩太矮了，够不到顶层架子上的书。

phrases.usage: 非常值得……，后面接名词或动名词，表付出的代价是值得的

examples.en: He feels that his rise in status is well worth the loss of money.
examples.zh: 他觉得地位提升了，损失这点钱是非常值得的。

examples.en: The view from the mountain top is well worth the two-hour climb.
examples.zh: 爬两个小时的山来看山顶的景色是非常值得的。

grammar.title: 定语从句 (Attributive Clause)
grammar.definition: 在复合句中修饰名词或代词的从句，用来对先行词进行修饰限定
grammar.structure: 先行词 + 关系代词/关系副词 + 从句剩余部分
grammar.usage: 用来补充说明先行词的特征、属性等，常用关系代词who指代人，which指代物，that既可指人或物

examples.en: People who do manual work often receive far more money than people who work in offices.
examples.zh: 从事体力劳动的人通常比坐办公室的人收入高得多。

examples.en: The book that you lent me last week is very interesting.
examples.zh: 你上周借给我的那本书非常有意思。

grammar.title: 将来进行时 (Future Continuous Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示将来某个时间正在进行的动作，或者将来某段时间内持续进行的动作
grammar.structure: will be + 动词的现在分词（doing）
grammar.usage: 用来表达对将来动作的客观描述，比一般将来时更强调动作在未来的持续性、进行状态

examples.en: He will soon be working in an office.
examples.zh: 他不久就要在办公室工作了。

examples.en: This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Paris.
examples.zh: 明天这个时候，我正飞往巴黎。

grammar.title: such引导的倒装句
grammar.definition: 为了强调表语部分，将such置于句首，句子需要部分倒装，用来加强语气
grammar.structure: Such + be + 主语 + that引导的结果状语从句
grammar.usage: 用来突出强调某事物的性质、特点，后接that从句说明该特点带来的结果

examples.en: Such is human nature, that a great many people are often willing to sacrifice higher pay for the privilege of becoming white-collar workers.
examples.zh: 很多人常常愿意牺牲更高的薪水来换取成为白领的优待，这就是人之常情。

examples.en: Such is the power of music that it can make people forget their sadness.
examples.zh: 这就是音乐的力量，它能让人们忘记悲伤。

sentencePatterns.pattern: A receive far more money than B

original.en: These days, people who do manual work often receive far more money than people who work in offices.
original.zh: 如今，从事体力劳动的人通常比坐办公室的人收入高得多。

imitations.en: Nowadays, skilled technicians often receive far more money than ordinary office clerks.
imitations.zh: 如今，技术娴熟的技工通常比普通办公室职员收入高得多。

imitations.en: In many cities, people who work in the catering industry often receive far more money than new graduates.
imitations.zh: 在很多城市，从事餐饮行业的人通常比刚毕业的学生收入高得多。

sentencePatterns.pattern: A be willing to sacrifice A for the privilege of B

original.en: A great many people are often willing to sacrifice higher pay for the privilege of becoming white-collar workers.
original.zh: 很多人常常愿意牺牲更高的薪水来换取成为白领的优待。

imitations.en: Many young people are willing to sacrifice their free time for the privilege of studying at a top university.
imitations.zh: 很多年轻人愿意牺牲自己的空闲时间来换取在顶尖大学学习的机会。

imitations.en: He was willing to sacrifice a stable job for the privilege of starting his own business.
imitations.zh: 他愿意牺牲一份稳定的工作来换取自己创业的机会。

sentencePatterns.pattern: A do this for + 时间段, and B keep the secret

original.en: Alf did this for over two years and his fellow dustmen kept his secret.
original.zh: 阿尔弗这样做了两年多，他的清洁工同事们都替他保守秘密。

imitations.en: She hid her illness from her family for three months and her close friends kept her secret.
imitations.zh: 她向家人隐瞒了自己的病情三个月，她的密友们都替她保守秘密。

imitations.en: He prepared a surprise birthday party for his wife for a week and all their common friends kept the secret.
imitations.zh: 他为妻子准备惊喜生日派对准备了一周，他们的共同好友都替他保守秘密。
